topic: long maceration and large neutral cask maturation

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A structured red undergoes extended skin contact and then matures in a large, often seasoned oak vessel.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Long maceration extracts substantial phenolics, while a large vessel has a lower wood-surface-to-wine ratio and therefore contributes less overt oak flavour than a small new barrel.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine retains firm structure and can evolve gradually without being dominated by new-oak aromas.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: long extraction builds structure and large casks permit maturation with restrained oak flavour.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
